triage.narrative: This triage focuses on high-severity vulnerabilities affecting the openssl-fips package within the kairos-io/hadron repository. These issues span multiple CVEs across 2024, 2025, and 2026, indicating a critical need for immediate patching.

This pull request only updates several indirect dependencies to newer versions. These types of dependency bumps are routine maintenance and do not introduce new security risks. The changes appear safe to merge automatically.

The changes are primarily feature additions (new matchers, plugin system) and internal refactoring aimed at improving robustness (cycle detection, iteration limits). There are no obvious security vulnerabilities introduced by these changes. The dependency update is a standard version bump, and the architectural changes in the SSH client appear to be performance/concurrency improvements. Therefore, this PR is safe to auto-approve.
reviews.changesSummary: This PR updates the `gomega` dependency to v1.42.1, introduces new matchers (`BeASlice`, `BeAnArray`), and adds a plugin system for Claude Code skills. It also includes significant internal refactoring to improve robustness, such as cycle detection in formatting and iteration limits in PKCS#12 decryption. Finally, it implements request pipelining for the SSH agent client to improve concurrency.
